subject: £16,000 compensation from your diesel
message:
Was A Cheat Device Fitted On Your Diesel Vehicle?
If you owned or leased a BMW, Ford, Renault, Jaguar, Land Rover, Range Rover, Mercedes, or Nissan diesel vehicle between 2009 and 2018, you may be eligible for compensation.

30 Second Vehicle Check
Check Now
Don't leave it to late - Check your vehicle now
	
Vehicle must have been manufactured and owned between 2009 - 2018
Car
	
Find affected vehicles in under 30 seconds, you don't need to still own the vehicle now
Customers
	
600,000 claims already processed & millions more vehicles affected - Claim now
Check Now
 

The Dieselgate scandal, which broke in 2015, revealed that Volkswagen had fitted millions of diesel cars—including around 1.2 million in the UK—with illegal software designed to cheat emissions tests. These “defeat devices” masked the true levels of harmful nitrogen oxide emissions during testing, misleading regulators and consumers. The scandal sparked legal action, government inquiries, and a shift in public perception of diesel vehicles across the UK, ultimately accelerating the move toward cleaner, more transparent automotive technologies.

Volkswagen settled the UK Dieselgate class action in May 2022 by agreeing to pay £193 million, worldwide, Dieselgate has cost VW over €30 billion, encompassing fines, recall costs, legal settlements, and buybacks.

Beyond Volkswagen, broader litigation continues: UK lawsuits could total billions, targeting emissions cheating by other manufacturers like Mercedes, Ford, BMW, Renault, Nissan, Jaguar, and Land Rover.
